analysis.name: Amy Pollock
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 205 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Amazing professor & very clear for those who may not understand chemistry well. Free book is a bonus. Very easy class. Participation and attendance required. Homework and recitation are "A for effort" grades. Short lectures. Fair amount of homework, but always short and easy. Easy tests if you study. If you did well in high school chem, you're fine

Amy is amazing. She is a super kind and a cute lady. However, if you do not come to the lectures, you will fail. Class material is basic chem and Amy teaches it super well. She is very passionate and explains every thing in great detail. There are about 2 easy homeworks a week.  Overall, if you come to class and pay attention, you should pass.

Dr. Pollock is a great professor and one of the best I've had at MSU. If you actually pay attention to the lectures then you will be fine. She makes chemistry easy to understand and a 4.0 is not very hard to get.

She is hands down one of the best professors that I ever had. The lectures were very easy to follow, but to understand the posted slides, you most often had to attend lecture. The exams tested your knowledge, but if you go and get outside help the exams are a piece of cake - and you can get extra credit. I would highly recommend her as a professor.

You need to attend lecture to understand anything, and then outside of lecture you need to look at the slides and make sure you understand what is going on. Study... a lot. The exams are nothing like what's done in lecture or recitation so make sure you look at the practice exams before hand and make sure you know how to do everything on them.

analysis.name: Amber Peters
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 27 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: To be honest her class is pretty easy. There are in class questions you must answer for attendance/grade, but class material is pretty easy. Exams are half multiple choice half written but again not hard. She is gone doing research a lot so will have canceled classes/ TA ran classes which is nice. My downfall was not paying attention in class

Dr. Peters is super nice! She taught half of the lectures, and Dr. Peacor taught the other half. I think that she had simpler topics, so it was easier to understand her. The rest of the class was quite difficult outside of her lectures. Something that made things a bit difficult was her lack of study materials for exams.

Dr. Peters is a great professor! She obviously knows what she's talking about and welcomes any questions or comments on the material. She makes an effort to get to know the class and lectures are engaging and interesting. Attendance is not required but participation matters! Exams are written response but easy if you go to class and participate.

I loved Dr. Peters! I enjoyed taking her Great Lakes Bio class. It wasn't a ton of work and the quizzes felt fair. This semester, she offered that if you had over a 90% in the class you didn't have to take the final exam so make sure you attend class! There were no attendance drops but a few chances for extra credit.

Dr. Peters is a good professor and she is obviously very knowledgeable in her field. However, her grading is weird sometimes and her quizzes/tests are all short answer. I would take her class again, but be prepared for it to be slightly unorganized and for her to be gone several lectures.

analysis.name: Ashley Moore
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward

review_highlights.comment: I loved Dr. Moore, and I had her for TE101. After reading negative reviews, it seems that people are upset due to the difficult discussions that were had in class, but Dr. Moore was always respectful to everyone while sharing her thoughts, and I never felt attacked or judged. She can be quite opinionated, but once you're used to it, it's all good.

I liked Ashley! She was super sweet and cared about our personal lives, would ask a group question at the start of every class. Class is only 50 min but flew by. Last 6ish weeks are student led presentations. Didn't ever really want to skip this class. Easy homework and the papers aren't bad at all. Would take class with her again any time!
Professor Ashley is caring, fun and genuine. I loved her class and woukd definitely take one of her classes again. She is very understanding and down to earth. Beautiful soul.
Removed a student from her class for difference of opinions, the TE 101 and 102 classes are a complete joke and waste of time and content.
Had us turn in a rough draft, and then graded it on accuracy. 0/10, wouldn't recommend, would choose a different teacher.

analysis.name: Alexandria Davis

review_highlights.comment: Alexandria is an awesome dance professor! She cares so much for her students and makes class like a fun workout! Totally recommend! I will keep taking her classes!

Alexandra is a kind person who is very passionate. But from a dancer perspective I feel I didn't learn much from what I thought the class was going to be. Along with that many things we did in class and talked about made me uncomfortable. If you are a dancer and wanting to dance I don't recommend

I love Alexandria! I am not a dancer by nature but I had a great time in her class. She really cares about your progress and is super understanding. I think I've really improved because of her. I would highly recommend her class

analysis.name: Sue Allen
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently

review_highlights.comment: Poor teaching style. Talks too much before class then rushes at the end, overall not organized in the slightest (which I need in a professor) her explanations are not cohesive/helpful. Treated class like high school, she's very funny but not a good math professor fit, she should be a highschool history teacher! If you're bad at math don't take her.

I didn't enjoy professor Allen's teaching style. She treated the class like a highschool class, and showed us the same sort of respect as highschoolers. She tends to start talking way before class started and usually ended class late. She was also sensitive about people leaving early. She was fine, but I didn't love her teaching style or anything.

such a difficult professor, so much to do on webwork but she says its not important. You only learn in lecture (not recitation), so if you miss the lecture or part of the lecture you're done for. She won't answer questions that she answered in class already, which made it feel like you couldn't email her with questions, also won't reteach concepts.

If you're good at math do other students a favor and take her lecture (people not good at math will struggle with her teaching style so let them take a better prof.) Lectures are too short for the amount of chatting she does. I retaught myself content after class. She's sweet and wants to help you when 1-on-1 but she should teach a smaller class.

She's a great teacher. I am terrible at math but I still managed a 3.0. I suggest attending all lectures and taking notes. Do webwork the same day as the lecture so it is easier. If you struggle GO TO OFFICE HOURS or MLC! She will help you with your homework and with your grades. She gives practice problems that aren't due, but really help.

analysis.name: Patrick Arnold

analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 20 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Professor Arnold is one of the most caring professors you will have at msu. They are very understanding and want you to succeed. There is an assignment due every other week that only takes about 30 minutes or less to do and 2 campus event reflections. As long as you complete your work the best you can, they will give you a 100!

Super nice and actually cares for students. The class itself is super easy and has discussion questions every other week which is really nice. Grading is super lenient and the assignments and readings are easy. Sometimes you do not even half to read the book to answer the discussion questions. Honors option is also easy.

i took this as an elective, easiest class ever. prof arnold is very sweet and such a nice person. this class requires little to no work. there's 1 online assignment a week that normally took ab 10-20 mins. u have to go to 2 events of ur choice during the semester. no tests/final. love prof arnold!!!

Professor Arnold is a great teacher and you can tell they genuinely care about their students. It's really easy to get a 4.0 as long as you complete the assignments. Probably the easiest class I've ever taken and doing the assignment each week didn't take much time.

Professor Arnolds class was amazing. I felt that the material was good and easy to understand, and they were extremely understanding for every single assignment that there was. There were also no exams! Overall I really liked this class.

Flaim is nice, and passionate. She cares for her students and her cause. However, she frequently goes off topic, which stops you from getting answers to your questions. She also grades harder than the other professors so you will probably have to work harder than your fellow classmates.
She is very understanding towards students and is passionate about her teaching.

Tough grader, all readings are similar and not interesting. The class is tailored for specific people. It is unorganized and good luck getting a 4.0 on a paper. Her grading makes absolutely no sense. Would not wish this on my worst enemy

analysis.name: Alan Arbogast
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 43 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: ISS205 is mainly a prerequisite, and the median grade across every professor for this course is a 4. However, he attempts to be different and unique by making this class harder than every other ISS. He believes a small percentage deserves a 4 even if everyone does well. This makes it completely unfair for his students compared to other professors

Really like the guy and his lectures, but I feel like he should make the lecture material actually available to the students instead of penalizing them for not retaining every word of what he says. His slides are basically just pictures, and are hard to make sense of after the class. His exams were fairly tough, but easy enough to get atleast an 80

Professor Alan is an all-around good guy, and I genuinely enjoyed waking up and going to his class. While the tests can be challenging sometimes, if you do the proper studying beforehand, then you should be well prepared. Always in a good mood and made learning interesting, relevant, and fun. Would highly recommend him to anyone.

The class is super lecture heavy, but the professor goes off on tangents that aren't relevant a lot so it's important you pay attention because his slides also don't give you a lot of information. He's a great person though and super nice and do the class readings!

Very very hard course for an ISS 200 level. He's an interactive and nice Prof. I would save yourself the trouble and take a different course. Way too hard to be a 200 level. You have zero assignments, class is based off 3 exams (including the final.) The exams can be tricky and require lots of studying. You must attend every class! No curve either!

analysis.name: Amanda Lorenz-Reaves
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 41 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: I could say the class is easy, or I could say that the material was explained so well and so well understood that the class seemed easy. Isn't that the goal as a good teacher to ensure students are learning all the material? Mission accomplished.

Clearly passionate about her lectures and it shows, they were all super easy to follow and interesting. The 2 Homework assignments are fun and don't take much time. In class participation assignments create interesting discussions, and students go to the MSU bug house at the end of the semester!

LOVEDDD Dr. Lorenz! She is very sweet and funny. This is an online class held over zoom so you'll have daily in class participation and pre-class readings, 3 home work, and 4 exams (all of which are open note). Genuinely enjoyed this class as her lectures are interesting and you can tell she is passionate about her work. Could not recommend enough!

I adored Professor Lorenz! She was incredibly gracious with RCPD extensions, and provided stress-free assignments. Every quiz and test was open resource, with plenty of time to complete the task. Lectures were engaging and interesting. She felicitated a calming learning environment while also sharing her passions. I cannot recommend enough!

Easiest ISB 201 class if you are willing to show up to class. She does in class papers for attendance (so easy you barely have to pay attention) basically your entire grade is attendance, a few 1 page papers & exams. The exams are very very easy. Super kind prof! Highly recommend her but dont get stuck in the 8:30am you wont make it.

analysis.name: Jan Brascamp
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 41 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Very chill professor, more than half way through the semester and we havent had a full length class yet. Answers questions through out lecture and his powerpoints are very well structured. You can tell he really cares about his students & is willing to help!

Brascamp is a very nice prof, and his class is pretty interesting. This class does have A LOT of content, so the exams can be a bit hard, but if you study, you will do fine. 60% of the lectures are mandatory for attending and there are studies you have to do, but they are easy. Exams are massive part of grade, so beware of that

This is one of the easiest psych classes I have ever taken. The professor doesn't go in-depth, which is nice since it keeps the exams really straightforward. It is test-heavy, but honestly, the questions are super easy if you actually study and understand the material. He uses PowerPoints, SO if you miss class, you can just read the slides.

I would recommend. Yes this class is hard and yes it is mainly lecture + exam-based. Brascamp's slides are amazing though, he is the first psych prof I've had that I genuinely learned from. He is passionate, gives tons of visuals, and explains very everything well. For exams, if you just study the slides and make a quizlet, u will be just fine.

His lectures are very heavy in content and he moves decently fast through the slides. He doesn't do much extra talking outside of what is written on the slide. Attendance isn't mandatory but you do get extra credit for going to class. There are no quizzes, just experiments and SONA. You'll definitely need to prepare for exams, but they're not bad.

analysis.name: Barb Gerber
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 18 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: TAKE THIS PROFESSOR! She is so nice and always cares for her students. She is very accessible outside of class and responds to emails very fast. She uses a sheet of paper to mark attendance and it does not matter if you are a couple minutes late to class. Would take her again and am convinced that she is the best teacher of this subject. Grade-100%
She is the best professor I had my freshman year. She is very caring and wants to see her students succeed. She is very flexible with grades.

Barb was a great professor. She really understands her students and the amount of work we have to do. We had deadlines on papers but she always moved them to about a week later. She took out a whole assignment because she thought we didnt have time. Definitely take her hybrid class, there was minimal work. And the papers were not even that long.

If you can, take the hybrid class because I do not see the point of going to a two hour writing class twice a week with the work that she assigns to us. Everything is very straight forward and most of her unit schedules are clear. I disliked how our assignments kept getting pushed back and things were graded late. She rarely responds to emails.

Barb SHOULD BE THE ONLY WRA PROFESSOR YOU TAKE! One time I forgot to turn in my paper by almost 3 weeks and she let it slide and gave me a full grade on it! Very nice and understanding, easy grader, easy assignments, easy 4.0, do not miss on the best opportunity to have the best WRA Professor in the world

This was Dr. Furqueron's first time teaching this class, it didn't seem like it at all. She makes the lectures super interesting, which cannot be said about most professors. She cares if you understand the material and is super helpful when it comes to questions. It is very easy to get points off on all of the assignments in here but overall she is an amazing professor.
Super amazing class would take agian.
Dr. Furqueron is quite actually the best instructor I have ever had! Her class is set up in a way where you really learn the material and it's enjoyable to go to class!!!!!!

all im gonna say is don't take this class, take literally any other class for your own future sanity. its interesting, but all that comes with this class is not worth it.

This class was so easy, and genuinely so fun and interesting. Attendance is not required, but I still looked forward to going because the class is amazing. I would 10/10 recommend Dr. Burnette. genuinely one of the best psych classes ive taken. dr. burnette is fantastic and the lectures are really well done and make class very interesting. i always looked forward to her class. all you have is weekly homework (which is straightforward and very manegabkle) and few exams. would highly reccomend!

analysis.name: Husain Khalifeh

analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: He is very mumbly and uses broken language so its difficult to follow when he "explains" programming, leaves class to take unnecessary calls, redundant advice which feels more interrogative than helpful, weirdly aggressive which I've never seen in another professor, and he couldn't solve his own problems and tried to hide his AI use at one point.

He's an amazing instructor and answers questions. If you did ANY programming in your life, the exams will be a piece of cake. He frequently looks over at your shoulder to see your code and tells you what is incorrect. The other reviewers who say that it's intimidating are saying it because they think their code is perfect and it's not.
The course basically killed my motivation to learn programming. Khalifeh is very slow, and has some speech problem (he keeps repeating same word/sentence 10 times). He is also very insensitive.

makes lots of programming and spelling errors in his labs. class was all self taught for me. His speaking is mumbled, unclear, and very broken English. Also quick to anger and gets into your personal space. He lectured me about paying attention in class for 5 minutes during a Timed quiz because I had one simple question to clarify instructions.
is not a kind person, struggles with technology, gets very close to students (especially females) and has very bad things to say about certain groups of students

analysis.name: Hezao Ke
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Really nice guy, but the class structure and assignments are really complex and confusing, and exams are HARD. Also this course in particular is very linguistics heavy with him as a prof, so keep that in mind.

I loved having Professor Ke, he was funny, engaging, and approachable. His lectures were clear and he was always willing to go over confusing information. The content itself can be challenging at times, but if you pay attention in class and ask questions during or after class, it's not difficult to do well in the course and genuinely learn a lot.
I'm taking IAH 231C and I would not recommend at all. None of the course information makes sense. He is a very nice professor, but the course material is dumb and confusing. DO NOT TAKE THIS COURSE

Professor Ke is an extremely kind person. Linguistics in general is horrible and hard to teach, but he tries to make up for it by being an easy grader. For assignments you have to record yourself to explain yourself, which was annoying, but trying to explain it out loud helped for understanding. Attend class or you will be confused.

Super nice, approachable professor and generally very open to suggestions and feedback. However, he wasn't always the clearest when explaining more complex concepts. The book wasn't really needed, but could be helpful when Alan's explanation just wasn't cutting it. Grading was generous and TONS of EC opportunities. Almost everthing was open-note.

analysis.name: Xinqiang Li
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: He seems super nice but he gives minimal criteria and zero guidance. His comments when grading an essay were “good” and that's it. He's nice but he doesn't teach you anything about writing and repeats himself a lot during the lecture.

He does not use D2L in a user-friendly way; it is nearly impossible to find materials, and he does not enter grades into the grade book, so you have no idea what the grade is for the whole class. During the two-hour lectures, he talks in circles and often will pick on someone to read because he does not want to. He is very nice but not the best.

Took his writing 101 course for this semester. He's a cool and super friendly teacher! Always willing to help you when you have questions. A lot of small homework but they only counted as the participation grade. 3 essay, 1 research paper, and 1 remix Video throughout the semester but they were really easy. Easy grader.
I like his class structure, especially the individual conference where I usually receive very helpful feedback.

Very easy professor, has lots of online/no classes due to one-on-one zoom conferences after each project assigned. However, he often gives unclear and bad feedback. Is a very easy grader but does NOT put grades into the grade book at all. There is no way to see your grade on d2l, and he has extremely boring lectures. Take if you want a 4. though

analysis.name: Lolo Robison
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: She cares for her students and their wellbeing. Requires attendance, Group project heavy!! Should be a hybrid class because of the number of times you get out of class early. Doesn't really grade anything so you won't know your grade until the end of semester.

Truly the best Prof I have had all of college- sees potential, passionate about projects, and incredibly hands on work! If you just show up and participate you will pass easily- she truly cares about the success of her students which is so refreshing.

Just take her courses, even for fun. Sometimes it can seem like a good amount of work but it is all so worth it and important info that will lead you to finding out more on your own. Her DEI course was great and very informative. LOVE her!!

This is my second class with Lolo, and she's great! My advice would be to participate actively in group discussions. She's great to have conversations with. You do most, if not all, work in groups but the work is easy
I loved Prof. Robison! She always let us out early, and the assignments were really interesting! The final is a group project, but it is very easy if you read the rubric.

analysis.name: Rachael Lund
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 35 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Rachael Lund is a great professor.  The calculus program at MSU is super difficult and the exams sometimes seem nearly impossible.  Rachael makes everything very clear and doesn't move on until everyone seems to understand the material.  I am not even a math person and I could follow everything going on.

GREAT PROFESSOR! This course was online and organized well. Everything was super easy to locate in D2L and help was easily accessible outside of class. You're given ample time to complete assignments, but I personally used an assignment tracker spreadsheet to help. Easy to excel in this class if you follow the guidance and manage your time well!

great professor! very organized d2l page that is easy to access and check off. it can be a lot considering the amount of videos and other assignments for the week like webwork ($50-55 for the semester), snapshots, and projects, but everything is set up monday and due the next wednesday which is great and lets me work on other things! no final exam!

great asynch class with very organized d2l that made it really easy to keep track of work. decent amount of work due every week and a half, but its manageble. my only complaint is some of the "tech tasks" and "investigations" were so unnecessarily long and arduous it got really annoying.

This class reminded me so much of the same way a high school class was like where the teacher actually helped you understand. But overall, the professor was very involved even though it was an online class they always provided office hours, so they did want you to succeed and pass but still understand the material that was being taught.

analysis.name: Lee Penyak
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: For a normal IAH requirement this class was insane. I spent most of my free time on this homework. Sometimes the homework did not relate to class content at all and everything was graded by AI so he couldn't explain why sometimes you would get a lower grade. His lectures would go in circles and test questions were just so specific and picky.

Dr. Penyak was delightful and you can see how much passion he has for this class through his lectures. He grades generally easy and while there is a lot of information to memorize for the tests, the tests themselves are very easy to pass and he applies a generous curve to each exam. His love for history makes the course very engaging.

Very interesting class, Professor is very knowledgeable on Mexican history with personal connections. Daily discussion posts that once you understand what he wants are easy to get good grades on. The tests aren't too hard, he also gave an extra credit. I would definitely take a class with him again.

Penyak is the best prof i have had. He is very smart with latin american everything, he is very interested and makes you intersted. He tells lots of cool stories and engages with students. Homework is easy to get good grades on, just put effort in. Exams were 80% MC and 20% definitions and very easy as long as you pay attention in class. great guy.

Overall Dr. Penyak is a very caring person, but the lectures are very boring and class is very content heavy. Attendance is mandatory, but the recitations were very helpful and the TA's are very nice. The exams are all about memorization and there is a lot of content to over.

analysis.name: lLina Qu
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 21 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: easy and super interesting class that only meets once a week. Just gotta go to class and raise your hand 6 times in a semester for full participation so easy again. Readings are through perusall which is annoying but again if you can't read 20 pages once a week you probably should not be in college. a few easy cooking assignments, great prof.

The class literally isn't that hard. Some of these reviews are just overreacting. That being said, I wouldn't take the class if you aren't into East Asia, which I feel like is pretty obvious already. There is only one lecture every week and one quiz every week with some projects and you need to participate 10 total times in class for the whole sem.

There is a weekly quiz on a movie and/or a reading every week which can be annoying. There are participation stickers and you need 10 to get 100%. Attendance is mandatory with two unexcused. Quizzes are not lock down. Has a midterm paper and final online presentation. Nice teacher, didn't have any problems.

Loved Dr. Qu! I'm honestly not sure why these reviews are so bad… her class is extremely easy. I got stuck with this class because it was the only IAH that fit into my schedule, however, I really enjoyed it. Class was only once a week and I actually found the lectures to be pretty interesting. Easy homework (quizzes) and midterm/final assignments.

Read the weekly course material and participate in class- the course is easy. People love to complain about everything. If you put effort into the essay portion of quiz you'll earn extra credit; I had no problems. 

I recommend this class to anyone genuinely interested in East Asia, b/c I enjoyed the material she provided. The lectures are boring.

analysis.name: Marty Spranger
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 476 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Spranger is very condescending. If a professor makes it a point to attend "study skills sessions" to get a better grasp of curriculum, they need to rethink your teaching style. Everyone learns differently and so much time was wasted pushing this on students instead of improving the way things are taught. I'm convinced the 5/5 reviews are bots.

Prof. Spranger comes off as a nice guy but he really is not. I study for hours on end, do everything he says that you need to do to get a 4.0, BS I get a 65 on every exam. Thanks to this man I am probably not going to get into nursing school. He is rude to students, often yelling at them and talking crap about them after class to the TA's

"I know this is..  a bit petty... I do not take this stuff too much to heart... However, when I went up for promotion last year for Associate Professor, a superior of mine mentioned that I get a lot of negative comments here.. 
You are a stellar student..."

He sent this email to only his glazers. THE REVIEWS HERE ARE SKEWED. Do better, Marty.

Dr. Spranger is very knowledgeable, but he expects WAY too much from students in an INTRODUCTORY Physiology class that is a university requirement. He expects you to set aside a ridiculous amount of time for a class that may not even be relevant for your major. Not impossible, but if his class isn't your main priority, it's so difficult.

By far the most miserable I've been in a class my 3 years in college. A lot of your grade depends on attendance and remembering parts of lectures he spends maybe 2 seconds going over. Had to email him over 16 times to solve an issue that he created and had 0 respect for students who have jobs/responsibilities/other classes.

analysis.name: Zenaida Moreno Mosqueda
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 26 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Prof. Moreno is very sweet and kind, and open to discussing social justice issues today. She is also very funny and loves to tell stories. This class is very writing-heavy, so just be prepared. She can be a harsh grader, but she does give good feedback so that you can get a good grade on the final draft. Overall, an amazing professor personally.

She was funny and nice to talk to, but was difficult when it came to grading. You need to be participating a lot during each class as she will take away points every lecture if not. The essays are worth 50% of your grade and the 2 tests had no study guide and we had no idea what would be on them. When I was sick I could not make up work missed

Professor Moreno Mosqueda was the worst professor I've experienced at MSU and many other people in our class agreed. It was almost impossible to get a 4.0 in her class unless you are a native Spanish speaker. She graded so harshly and then offered no further help or feedback. She was rude at times and very inaccessible outside of class for help.

Absolutely dreaded this class every week because of her. Consistently complained about her job, was impossible to reach via email and for office hours, and docked attendance points even if you participated and were paying attention. Showed no understanding for her students, lacked professionalism, and was condescending and rude multiple times.

Overall, the class content was not difficult. Her grading was harsh and she was condescending. For a language class, we should be given room to learn from our mistakes and improve them. However, Professor Moreno is unforgiving. Did not seem like she cared.

analysis.name: Mohamed Satti
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 12 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: I would recommend going to one of his office hours to introduce yourself or ask questions, he's a really nice guy to talk to. I would not say that this class has a heavy workload. We usually had 1-2 assignments a week along with some reading. Lectures are easy to understand and the exams are directly from learning actives in class.

You're on your own in this course. Despite emailing Professor Satti for help multiple times throughout the semester, his responses were short and sharp, and it is clear that he is unfit to be teaching an asynchronous class.

While this class is all online, Dr. Satti was truly a great professor. Many of the aspects of this course are outside of his control, however, when asking for clarification or you need a better understanding of the content, he truly was knowledgeable and cared about whether you understood the concept. Strongly recommend Dr. Satti!

Get ready to question your sanity as you embark on the horrendous journey of PH101. You will spend hours every day if you wish to do the required readings (which you should take notes on if you are seeking a good grade on quizzes and assignments) and trying to complete some very complicated writing assignments. You will be so frustrated every week.

So my roomie took this course, and bro legit stole her from me. They were losing sleep trying to put in the most insane amount of time ever requested by a teacher for a non-honours, online 100 level course (literally multiple hours a day). It's absurd. They read this whack book that had some really disturbing content and no content warning???

analysis.name: Mariana Medina
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 47 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Professor Medina is kind and cares about her students. This class was easy, and as long as you showed up to recitation, you could get a great grade on every assignment. No final or midterms. Although reading was required, it wasn't necessary to excel in the course. Great professor, and would be happy to take another one of her classes!

You could tell Dr. Medina was very interested in the subject matter, and knew a lot about it, so this made asking her questions or whatnot a lot easier. You're only graded by a few things, but she makes the grading criteria very flexible, and she's a pretty easy grader. Definitely take the honors section if you need an honors credit!

Take this professor if you can! She is very nice and caring! Grades are comprised of weekly assignments that you do in the recitation and attendance, so no exams or anything. The class was super easy, and make sure to go to the recitation! Really nice prof, great at helping with honors credit as well!

Objectively easy course. The lectures are boring but there's no exams, you just need to show up for attendance, and you can skim the powerpoint to get an easy 3.0+. She's very understanding and the absence policy is pretty lenient. Great GPA boost/course to add onto a busy schedule. Would take again!

Great prof who really cares about her students! Attendance policy is super lenient, and I found that it wasn't hard to catch up if you missed class. Quizzes were all open-book, open-lecture and open-note (I got a 4.0 just from Ctrl + F searching key terms), and there are no midterms or finals -- your entire grade is 20% attendance, 80% quizzes.

analysis.name: Nicole Mccleese
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 24 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: McCleese only gave out 5 assignments the whole semester.  She takes attendance randomly so just attend even though it is boring af. Her lessons are so boring, playing minesweeper is more entertaining. She reads everything off of a slideshow, (which that is NOT posted to D2L).

Slow grades (no grades entered more than halfway through the semester), and the course topic is just not what the class is described as. If you want to learn more about Chilean politics, def take this course, but if you're looking for reading writing by women, this isn't it.

One of the worst professors I've ever had. I was expecting the course to relate to the description MSU put out, but she put an "indigenous food studies" twist to it. Lectures consist of her reading slides in a monotone voice. She gives us useless assingments and hasn't graded anything the entire semester (less than a month left). Stay away.

Very careless about student feedback or incidents. Took random attendance for the first month as a scare tactic, then suddenly stopped, nobody knows when she will take attendance next. If you are absent, she gives 0 grace to your situation regardless of how serious. She has a temper, and petty mannerisms. There's not much work she gives out.

This professor graded everything a week before the course ended. An okay instructor who oddly made the class about food culture. Assignments were easy ten-minute tasks, with only five total grades, so submit on time. Attendance wasn't taken, so skipping didn't matter. Clear grading, but never replied to emails.

analysis.name: Stephanie Nawyn
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 35 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: I really enjoyed Professor Nawyn's lectures. She's funny and explains terms/theories well. She has online open note exams, weekly readings, and iclicker quizzes, but they're fairly easy if you read the textbook and pay attention in class. Would 100% recommend taking SOC 100 with her!

All exams are open note/book, super easy class overall and hard not to 4.0! Lectures can be long/boring, she tends to get off task. The sweetest professor-intelligent, understanding, down to earth, absolutely hilarious. She wants everyone to succeed. I wish I could get coffee with her. Couldn't recommend enough!

Such a wonderful professor to have. She is educated, thoughtful, passionate, and caring to her students. She truly does care, and you can just tell. There were five exams, with the final, and one can be dropped. All are open note, and most were taken online, with an in-person option. No reason not to take a class with such a wonderful person :)

I love Prof Nawyn. There are iclicker questions and daily quizzes but they are easy as long as you do the readings which are a little long but its fine. Inquisitives were annoying but easy points. Very fun and followable lectures, shes funny here and there too. Very accommodating and there are many points in the class. Easy exams w a note card.

This is the easiest class I have taken. The lectures are important to go to for extra credit, lecture points and for exam material. There is so much extra credit given that I had 20 points of extra credit over the maximum points available in the class. You get a cheat sheet on the test and the beginning of lecture quizzes are just vocab.

analysis.name: Thomas Nikolai
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Nice, easy-going guy who you can tell really enjoys the content. No additional materials needed besides the posted lectures. My one warning is that the class isn't a slam-dunk 4.0 like the name would make you think, and the 3 unit tests + final (although they're open note) will sometimes touch on some of the more obscure points from lectures.

I took this along with World of Turf and I actually prefer this class over that one. The topics were interesting, the lectures were engaging and humorous (occasionally), and the professor actually cares for his students. Throughout the semester, he would give us so many updates/reminders. 4 total exams (final included) and 1 is dropped!

I recommend that everyone take this class! It's such a good class when you just need a few extra credits but don't want something difficult. Your grade is entirely based off of quizzes and tests but as long as you look over the lectures they are super easy. He makes the class very enjoyable and writes the nicest emails every week!

Honestly, an amazing professor. He's fully online and just has quizzes throughout the week that you have like a month to complete. I didn't even have to take the final because he only makes you keep 3 of the exams out of the 4. Also a really funny guy.
Insanely easy 4.0, he wants you to succeed.

analysis.name: Nicholas Ovenhouse
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: One of the best math professors I've had. His lectures are very clear and simple making them not hard to follow along. His office hours were the main reason I got A's on the tests so I highly recommend going to them if you ever need help on the content or just for review before a test. He is always very kind and helpful whenever you talk to him.

Calc 3 is one of the hardest classes I've taken at Michigan State, but the main difference I vastly prefer it over Calc 2 is because of Professor Ovenhouse. His lectures were always super detailed and he always went over good examples, which prepared you really well for homework and exams. 10/10.
He was good at explaining tough concepts. I didn't get the best grade because I missed some lectures towards the end but every time I attended I was able to understand the concepts, very nice guy.
He is really good and the class in really exam heavy but as long as you come to lecture and understand the material the class is light.

Nick is a great teacher. He obviously loves his subject and wants his students to do well. He's very organized and will answer any questions you have in a manner that benefits you as a student. He'll go over homework assignments to make sure you can complete them. The only downside is Lab Day - you'll have to work in groups on a large worksheet.

analysis.name: Rola Nashef
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: If you take any of her classes, be ready to stay on top of things yourself. The D2L page can be difficult to navigate, and assignments aren't always clearly communicated in class. The schedule changes frequently, and lectures often run long with a lot of time spent on extended presentations.

Professor Nashef is honestly one of my favorite professors at MSU. She genuinely cares about her students and their growth throughout the class, and she gives insightful lectures. She also brings incredible experience in the field. I would definitely take another class with her again, overall an easy class.

Taking this class was simply annoying, she would lecture for two hours by repeating the same thing over and over and would have a needed thought until the class was ending and tell us to wait and listen to her. She never gave very good advice to improve our work and if we didn't want to do exactly what she wanted she would seem angry and upset.

Writing this review is the reason that I got an account on RMP. There are only so many words that will fit into this review, but my main issue is that she doesn't give feedback that will help one grow as a screenwriter. I rewrote my script from scratch just because she didn't like the story I was trying to tell, rather than the writing itself.
Professor Nashef is a very nice person who seems to really care about their students. The class is very fun too!

analysis.name: Nicole Geske
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 19 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Doesn't seem to care much if students are doing well. 2-week turnaround b/w exams and review sessions are A JOKE. They won't tell you the questions you got wrong, so you're not able to learn from them. This entire course was run terribly, and when everyone was doing poorly, they blamed us and took 0 accountability. Absolutely ludicrous.

If you are good at fast memorization, you'll be fine. Content is taught solely online through an interactive textbook (with graded questions), and exams are usually every other week. No final! Take notes only on the learning objectives (skip unrelated content), and memorize them all.

I took the anatomy lab with Dr.Geske so I worked with her in person. She was not very instructive during this class. When we had questions, we tried to get help from the other profs because they were much more helpful. This class is nearly impossible if you don't spend every spare minute you have teaching yourself everything. I
Got to be one of the worst ways to teach a class.

All you do is read the textbook and teach yourself. Exams are impossible. There are review sessions but she makes them in the middle of the day so ppl with other classes cannot attend which is not fair. Is not open to suggestions and its petty when you ask if the review sessions can be move so more students can utilize them. DO NOT TAKE!!!

analysis.name: Sebnem Onsay
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 28 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Very poorly structured & disorganized course. Too much material: unnecessarily repetitive with videos, zybooks, lectures covering the same content. Took WAY more time than my other classes combined. D2L is a mess, with content all over the place. Each project is 1% & each exam is 17% of your grade. Def NOT a 3 credit workload, more like 5 credits.

One of the nicest professors I have had, very helpful and knowledgeable. Not a good teacher though, lectures are hard to sit through and lecture slides are 100+ slides long every day making it almost impossible to study them or use them for projects.

Class was poorly structured with project quizzes usually happening 1-2 weeks after the project was due and quiz questions being generally confusing. But it wasn't that difficult, so it's fine. I don't appreciate the exams being outside of scheduled lecture times.

Bro she a GOAT. The course is chill, a lot of working but if you just see her videos and then go to lecture you'll be fine. She understands you got stuff going. She chill and tbh you can really vibe with her if you enjoy DSA like she does. Also she always looking for TAs so if you wanna be one just participate and make yourself known

The professor is great, and you can tell she cares about the students. My only complaint is that the class has tons of material, and she does a terrible job of organizing things on D2L. It is such a mess and hard to study for an exam. The concepts are fairly easy but she tends to overcomplicate them when explaining in class.

analysis.name: Osvaldo Hernandez
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 52 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: You think that you understand the material, you study the study guides he gives you, then you get the exam and are mind blown because it is nothing like you expected! Super hard exams that is for sure. Really boring lectures, too. Randomly gives in class work for credit so you should definitely go to lecture.

Such a nice professor!!! You also can tell how much he loves teaching this. Made class interesting enough for people who don't like science. Group projects that weren't awful although some days were more boring than others. Mandatory attendance but he usually lets us go early.

Lectures were boring as I'm not passionate about the environment, but Ozzy is a caring professor and an interesting person. Attendance is based on short questions you have to turn in online after class, and exams were online for the winter/spring '25 semester. He understands outside circumstances and will be lenient as long as you have reason.
The TAs teach the course. I've never even met the main professor. If you get a good TA, then you will get a good grade. Luckily, mine was amazing!

In class assignments you can do from home, lectures are SO hard to sit through. three exams that were all taken online outside of class, not proctored. I would recommend to others for integrative studies requirement. very easy coursework that takes less than 10 mins. not a lot of homework at all

analysis.name: Olufunmbi Elemo
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment:
MC377: Culture, Politics, and Postcolonialism: Profiling Sub-Saharan Africa
Here's the tea: Elemo is VERY supportive in providing resources and suggestions for final projects and large essays. Has engaging lectures and values class participation. Communication is key, highly recommend attending office hours. E-K is a great prof for this subject.

I had Professor Elemo for MC 201 and am currently taking her MC 492 class. She does an excellent job explaining complex readings and breaking them down clearly. She is thoughtful, supportive, and genuinely cares about her students' success.

Professor Elemo's truly one of my favorite JMC professors. I had some medical issues towards the end of the semester and she was extremely understanding when it came to my absences (unlike some other professors). She's truly passionate about what she's teaching and talking to her about class material is extremely beneficial. Highly recommend!

I had her for MC 201. Though I didn't like her class my freshman year, I realized she's actually an amazing professor in MC325! She has amazing lectures, helpful during office hours, and overall a very understanding professor if you show up. Attendance is very important but you're allowed four absences which is much more than other professors.

Contrary to popular belief, she's a very good prof. Her tone may be condescending at times, but she teaches well and has clear expectations. She is a tough grader but provides good advice. Just take her feedback into account and go to office hours if you're struggling and you'll be fine.

analysis.name: Orlando Hawkins
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: My favorite JMC prof I've had thus far, he creates a comfy environment. I used to be so nervous to join discussions, but I was good to share my takes in this class. He gives genuine feedback, and will happily meet with you if you need help. He also lectures with slides instead of just yapping for a straight hour and a half. Pls take him he's great.

Professor Hawkins is a refreshing change in the PTCD lineup. He has moved away from the usual Straussian dogma toward a more critical, historically grounded approach. If you're a Straussian, you may not enjoy the critical readings so much, but there will be ample opportunity to interpret and grapple with the canonical texts. Participating is key!
